tweets.title: Around 50 police personnel were reportedly injured today, some critically. A roughly similar number of civilians were also injured. I have a few requests for Delhi Police, the Home Ministry, and other concerned agencies: • Identify those involved who were not students. The agencies have the tools to do this quickly. Please exercise restraint with genuine students and avoid indiscriminate cases against them. • Anyone associated with political parties who participated in unlawful activity should be identified and prosecuted strictly according to law. • Publish an official, verified tally of injuries, arrests, and detentions. There is already a massive wave of propaganda and conflicting claims.
tweets.content:
Around 50 police personnel were reportedly injured today, some critically. A roughly similar number of civilians were also injured.

I have a few requests for Delhi Police, the Home Ministry, and other concerned agencies:

• Identify those involved who were not students. The agencies have the tools to do this quickly. Please exercise restraint with genuine students and avoid indiscriminate cases against them.

• Anyone associated with political parties who participated in unlawful activity should be identified and prosecuted strictly according to law.

• Publish an official, verified tally of injuries, arrests, and detentions. There is already a massive wave of propaganda and conflicting claims.
tweets.author: @elitepredatorss

tweets.title: R to @sidhant: Ukraine foreign minister:
tweets.content:
Ukraine foreign minister:

Andrii Sybiha 🇺🇦 (@andrii_sybiha)

While the world is still responding to Russia's brutal overnight attack on Kyiv, Moscow continues its campaign of terror throughout the day.

Russia has now struck a civilian vessel navigating Ukraine's maritime corridor. The crew included citizens of Syria and India. A Ukrainian maritime pilot was killed, as well as four crew members, eight crew members were evacuated and five still remain missing.

A civilian ship. An international crew. A route serving global food security. Putin saw another target.

This attack leaves no room for illusions. Putin's Russia does not care whom it kills. Ukrainians or citizens of any other nation. For Putin, human life does not matter. Only his criminal objectives do.

In the last wave of attacks, it wasn’t an isolated incident. Just yesterday, Russia attacked another foreign merchant vessel in the Black Sea flying the flag of Antigua and Barbuda, killing one person and injuring three others. Russia is deliberately turning freedom of navigation into a target of war.

Upon Prime Minister @KoretskyiUA’s instructions, @MFA_Ukraine is already informing the International Maritime Organization and our international partners about Russia's latest attacks on civilian shipping, as well as the governments of the states whose citizens were killed or injured.

During President @ZelenskyyUa's recent visit to London, I discussed this issue with the IMO leadership and called for the deployment of an international monitoring mission to Ukraine's ports. We expect this mission to begin its work as planned in the fall and help document every act of Russia's maritime terror, paving the way for accountability.

Today Russia also struck the Kharkiv region with missiles, killing at least four people and injuring at least twenty. In Zaporizhzhia, guided aerial bombs killed at least three civilians, injured at least thirty three more.

Every delay in increasing pressure on Moscow costs more innocent lives. Russia must face overwhelming force, crippling sanctions, lower oil revenues, full diplomatic isolation, and justice. Terror must never be allowed to prevail.

— https://nitter.net/andrii_sybiha/status/2078903848878350409#m
tweets.author: @sidhant

tweets.title: Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief: 20th Jul 2026: - We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East - ⁠Follow us for daily Brief ● Bahrain reported that Iranian drones attempted to target and disrupt its civil air navigation systems, though air traffic remained operational. ● Kuwaiti air defenses intercepted hostile drone waves, though local power and water desalination facilities suffered hits and fires. ● IRGC announced that large C-17 transport aircraft and P-8 command and control aircraft of the invading American army at Aqaba Airport in Jordan were targeted. ● IRGC said 2 oil tankers exploded and were left disabled after attempting to transit the southern Strait of Hormuz through an unauthorized and unsafe route. ● Iranian Interior Minister Eskandar Momeni left Tehran for Islamabad at the invitation of his Pakistani counterpart Mohsin Naqvi. ● Iran called on the UN nuclear watchdog to explicitly condemn a recent US attack on Darkhoveyn nuclear power plant construction site. END OF THE BRIEF
tweets.content:
Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
20th Jul 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● Bahrain reported that Iranian drones attempted to target and disrupt its civil air navigation systems, though air traffic remained operational.

● Kuwaiti air defenses intercepted hostile drone waves, though local power and water desalination facilities suffered hits and fires.

● IRGC announced that large C-17 transport aircraft and P-8 command and control aircraft of the invading American army at Aqaba Airport in Jordan were targeted.

● IRGC said 2 oil tankers exploded and were left disabled after attempting to transit the southern Strait of Hormuz through an unauthorized and unsafe route.

● Iranian Interior Minister Eskandar Momeni left Tehran for Islamabad at the invitation of his Pakistani counterpart Mohsin Naqvi.

● Iran called on the UN nuclear watchdog to explicitly condemn a recent US attack on Darkhoveyn nuclear power plant construction site.

END OF THE BRIEF

Netram Defence Review (@NetramDefence)

Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
19th Jul 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● The U.S. military launched an eighth consecutive night of airstrikes against Iran, specifically targeting the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps. These strikes are in retaliation for an Iranian attack in Jordan that resulted in the deaths of two American service members and left another missing.

● The U.S. strikes targeted Iranian coastal surveillance, air defense facilities, and missile and drone storage sites. The operation aims to degrade Iran's ability to restrict commercial shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.

● Iran launched missiles toward the Jordanian city of Aqaba, which were intercepted by Jordanian and Israeli forces. In response, Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz warned that Israel would respond with "full force" to any Iranian attacks.

● Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister announced the suspension of Tehran's commitments to an interim memorandum of understanding, stating that the U.S. had violated the agreement.

● U.S. President Donald Trump is reportedly considering further military escalation against Iran, as diplomatic solutions are now assessed to be virtually nonexistent.

END OF THE BRIEF

— https://nitter.net/NetramDefence/status/2078919199918092595#m
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title: Days ahead of Kargil Vijay Diwas, senior Pakistani politician and MP Maulana Fazal-ur-Rehman has launched a direct public attack on the Pakistan Army regarding its conduct during the 1999 Kargil War with India. Rehman alleged that the military leadership deliberately concealed its casualties. He stated that the Pakistan Army refused to acknowledge its fallen soldiers, whose numbers ran into the hundreds, and initially left their bodies behind. He further accused the military of disrespecting its own ranks, claiming the army has never released a public list of the Kargil casualties and is now using the sacrifices of its soldiers merely as a shield to cover up its own strategic and political failures.
tweets.content:
Days ahead of Kargil Vijay Diwas, senior Pakistani politician and MP Maulana Fazal-ur-Rehman has launched a direct public attack on the Pakistan Army regarding its conduct during the 1999 Kargil War with India.

Rehman alleged that the military leadership deliberately concealed its casualties. He stated that the Pakistan Army refused to acknowledge its fallen soldiers, whose numbers ran into the hundreds, and initially left their bodies behind.

He further accused the military of disrespecting its own ranks, claiming the army has never released a public list of the Kargil casualties and is now using the sacrifices of its soldiers merely as a shield to cover up its own strategic and political failures.

tweets.title: Come on, be responsible with what you post. We expect maturity from educators, at least. That’s a QRT, or Quick Reaction Team (evident from the vehicle). A QRT responds to any kind of threat that needs to be neutralised and contained immediately, as the name suggests. They are following their SOPs to secure the gate. It doesn’t matter whether protestors are coming through or terrorists. Major installations have multiple QRTs assigned to different yet specific tasks. Kindly cross-check such things before you post, either with your friends in the police/armed forces or with people who have subject-matter knowledge. You know that a post like this can fuel unnecessary distrust among less-informed people.
tweets.content:
Come on, be responsible with what you post. We expect maturity from educators, at least.

That’s a QRT, or Quick Reaction Team (evident from the vehicle). A QRT responds to any kind of threat that needs to be neutralised and contained immediately, as the name suggests.

They are following their SOPs to secure the gate. It doesn’t matter whether protestors are coming through or terrorists.

tweets.title: Yes, dependence on the Israeli OEM for certain radar modes is a legitimate concern, and ideally the aircraft should have received the Uttam AESA from the outset. However, Uttam is planned for Batch 2, where it is also expected to feature GaN technology. Hopefully, once Virupaksha matures, we will see a GaN-based “steering” AESA transition for the Tejas Mk2 production standard as well. That said, taunts like “hangar beauty” should be taken on the chin. Manufacturing and capability development is difficult to process for those who aren’t associated with the domain. Let the import bahadurs win today’s narrative so India can win more important war of Atmanirbharta. A year from now, the picture will likely look very different.
tweets.content:
Yes, dependence on the Israeli OEM for certain radar modes is a legitimate concern, and ideally the aircraft should have received the Uttam AESA from the outset. However, Uttam is planned for Batch 2, where it is also expected to feature GaN technology. Hopefully, once Virupaksha matures, we will see a GaN-based “steering” AESA transition for the Tejas Mk2 production standard as well.

That said, taunts like “hangar beauty” should be taken on the chin. Manufacturing and capability development is difficult to process for those who aren’t associated with the domain. Let the import bahadurs win today’s narrative so India can win more important war of Atmanirbharta.

A year from now, the picture will likely look very different.
tweets.author: @alpha_defense

tweets.title: Centre Plans Bill to Criminalise Disruption of 'Vande Mataram' The Centre is set to introduce the Prevention of Insults to National Honour (Amendment) Bill, 2026 during the Monsoon Session of Parliament. If passed, the Bill will make intentionally preventing or disrupting the singing of 'Vande Mataram' punishable with up to 3 years' imprisonment, a fine, or both, bringing the national song under the same legal protection currently granted to the National Anthem. The proposed law also states that repeat offenders could face a minimum one-year jail term. It further mandates that 'Vande Mataram' be sung in full at all state functions and civil investiture ceremonies.
tweets.content:
Centre Plans Bill to Criminalise Disruption of 'Vande Mataram'

The Centre is set to introduce the Prevention of Insults to National Honour (Amendment) Bill, 2026 during the Monsoon Session of Parliament.

If passed, the Bill will make intentionally preventing or disrupting the singing of 'Vande Mataram' punishable with up to 3 years' imprisonment, a fine, or both, bringing the national song under the same legal protection currently granted to the National Anthem.

The proposed law also states that repeat offenders could face a minimum one-year jail term. It further mandates that 'Vande Mataram' be sung in full at all state functions and civil investiture ceremonies.

tweets.title: DELHI POLICE: No Permission for Parliament March, Imposes Section 163 BNS (formerly Sec 144 IPC) Ahead of the Monsoon Session, Delhi Police said no permission has been sought or granted for the proposed CJP "Chalo Sansad" march on 20 July. Police said Section 163 BNSS prohibitory orders are in force across New Delhi, prohibiting unauthorised processions and gatherings. Authorities warned that any violation could invite legal action under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) and other applicable laws. Security has been tightened in the national capital for the commencement of Parliament's Monsoon Session.
tweets.content:
DELHI POLICE: No Permission for Parliament March, Imposes Section 163 BNS (formerly Sec 144 IPC)

Ahead of the Monsoon Session, Delhi Police said no permission has been sought or granted for the proposed CJP "Chalo Sansad" march on 20 July.

Police said Section 163 BNSS prohibitory orders are in force across New Delhi, prohibiting unauthorised processions and gatherings. Authorities warned that any violation could invite legal action under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) and other applicable laws.

Security has been tightened in the national capital for the commencement of Parliament's Monsoon Session.

tweets.title: Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief: 19th Jul 2026: - We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East - ⁠Follow us for daily Brief ● The U.S. military launched an eighth consecutive night of airstrikes against Iran, specifically targeting the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps. These strikes are in retaliation for an Iranian attack in Jordan that resulted in the deaths of two American service members and left another missing. ● The U.S. strikes targeted Iranian coastal surveillance, air defense facilities, and missile and drone storage sites. The operation aims to degrade Iran's ability to restrict commercial shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz. ● Iran launched missiles toward the Jordanian city of Aqaba, which were intercepted by Jordanian and Israeli forces. In response, Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz warned that Israel would respond with "full force" to any Iranian attacks. ● Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister announced the suspension of Tehran's commitments to an interim memorandum of understanding, stating that the U.S. had violated the agreement. ● U.S. President Donald Trump is reportedly considering further military escalation against Iran, as diplomatic solutions are now assessed to be virtually nonexistent. END OF THE BRIEF
tweets.content:
Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
19th Jul 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● The U.S. military launched an eighth consecutive night of airstrikes against Iran, specifically targeting the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps. These strikes are in retaliation for an Iranian attack in Jordan that resulted in the deaths of two American service members and left another missing.

● The U.S. strikes targeted Iranian coastal surveillance, air defense facilities, and missile and drone storage sites. The operation aims to degrade Iran's ability to restrict commercial shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.

● Iran launched missiles toward the Jordanian city of Aqaba, which were intercepted by Jordanian and Israeli forces. In response, Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz warned that Israel would respond with "full force" to any Iranian attacks.

● Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister announced the suspension of Tehran's commitments to an interim memorandum of understanding, stating that the U.S. had violated the agreement.

● U.S. President Donald Trump is reportedly considering further military escalation against Iran, as diplomatic solutions are now assessed to be virtually nonexistent.

END OF THE BRIEF

Netram Defence Review (@NetramDefence)

Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
18th Jul 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● IDF forces identified and struck a Hezbollah terrorist cell that had been operating drones and taking cover near IDF soldiers in the Security Zone.

● IRGC said they carried out a missile & drone attack on fighter shelters and a large aircraft parking apron at a US base in Azraq, Jordan, destroying at least 2 fighter jets and 3 other US aircraft.

● Kuwait Petroleum Corporation said one of its oil facilities was hit by repeated Iranian attacks, causing significant damage and injuring several workers.

● US attacks overnight damaged a bridge on the Bandar Abbas-Hajjiabad axis and caused destruction at four points along major roadways in Hormozgan province.

● Iran suspended its commitments under the Islamabad MoU with the US after Washington "violated all of its obligations" under the agreement.

END OF THE BRIEF

— https://nitter.net/NetramDefence/status/2078551394039324951#m

They have been urged to remain vigilant, follow safety guidelines and contact the administration immediately in case of any emergency.
tweets.content:
The Indian Armed Forces forces are leaving no stone unturned to secure the citizens.

Officials said all nomadic families in the sub-division have been mapped and asked to avoid camping near nallahs, water channels and landslide-prone locations for the next one week. They have been urged to remain vigilant, follow safety guidelines and contact the administration immediately in case of any emergency.

tweets.title: India Plans First-Ever Border Fence Through the Sundarbans India is planning to construct its first border fence through the Sundarbans along the Bangladesh frontier, covering approximately 90 km in West Bengal. According to reports, the BSF has begun feasibility and technical studies for the project. The challenging riverine border is currently secured through Floating Border Outposts, marine patrols, and surveillance, with plans to further strengthen security infrastructure. The proposed fencing faces logistical and environmental challenges, with 71 km of the border adjoining the protected Sundarbans Wildlife Sanctuary, where construction is restricted. Authorities have assured compensation and rehabilitation to affected residents if land acquisition is required. The project is currently in the planning and assessment stage, with no construction timeline officially announced.
tweets.content:
India Plans First-Ever Border Fence Through the Sundarbans

India is planning to construct its first border fence through the Sundarbans along the Bangladesh frontier, covering approximately 90 km in West Bengal.

According to reports, the BSF has begun feasibility and technical studies for the project. The challenging riverine border is currently secured through Floating Border Outposts, marine patrols, and surveillance, with plans to further strengthen security infrastructure.

The proposed fencing faces logistical and environmental challenges, with 71 km of the border adjoining the protected Sundarbans Wildlife Sanctuary, where construction is restricted. Authorities have assured compensation and rehabilitation to affected residents if land acquisition is required.

The project is currently in the planning and assessment stage, with no construction timeline officially announced.
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title: BREAKING: US Launches Fresh Strikes on Iran After Deadly Attack on American Troops The United States has launched a new round of airstrikes against Iran after two U.S. service members were killed and another reported missing following an Iranian attack in Jordan. According to U.S. Central Command, the strikes were ordered by President Donald Trump and targeted IRGC military infrastructure, logistics, surveillance sites, underground weapons storage, and maritime capabilities to degrade Iran's ability to threaten shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. Iranian media reported a U.S. strike near Sirik in southern Iran but claimed there were no casualties or major infrastructure damage.
tweets.content:
BREAKING: US Launches Fresh Strikes on Iran After Deadly Attack on American Troops

The United States has launched a new round of airstrikes against Iran after two U.S. service members were killed and another reported missing following an Iranian attack in Jordan.

According to U.S. Central Command, the strikes were ordered by President Donald Trump and targeted IRGC military infrastructure, logistics, surveillance sites, underground weapons storage, and maritime capabilities to degrade Iran's ability to threaten shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

Iranian media reported a U.S. strike near Sirik in southern Iran but claimed there were no casualties or major infrastructure damage.

tweets.title: A humongous rescue effort was launched by the Indian Army, J&K Police, and Central Armed Police Forces. The upper regions of the valley have been hit by severe floods. It might sound routine for the armed forces to everyone, but the sheer coordination required to execute such an operation within hours is something that serves as a masterclass in management. The troops simultaneously make their own units safe while continuing to rescue civilians. Alongside this, search operations are also launched for people reported missing due to the severity of the floods.
tweets.content:
A humongous rescue effort was launched by the Indian Army, J&K Police, and Central Armed Police Forces.

The upper regions of the valley have been hit by severe floods.

It might sound routine for the armed forces to everyone, but the sheer coordination required to execute such an operation within hours is something that serves as a masterclass in management.

The troops simultaneously make their own units safe while continuing to rescue civilians. Alongside this, search operations are also launched for people reported missing due to the severity of the floods.
